title: <name> and the Secret of the Singing Waterfall

pages.text: It was a bright morning in the foothills of the Himalayas. The air smelled of pine trees, and the mountains looked like they were wearing soft white caps of snow. <name> and her classmates had come for a school picnic near the river Ganga.

“Let’s race to the big rock!” shouted her friend Mehul. As they ran, <name> noticed strange markings on the rock — swirls and arrows carved into its surface. “Hey, look!” she said. “It looks like a map!”
Her teacher, Miss Renu, smiled. “Maybe it’s an old trail map from long ago.” But <name>’s curiosity sparked. “Can we follow it, Ma’am? Please?”
After lunch, Miss Renu agreed to a short hike. The children followed the arrows through tall grass and whispering trees. “Listen!” said Mehul. “Do you hear that sound?”

It was a whoosh-shhh noise, like someone singing softly. They walked faster until they reached a cluster of rocks. Behind them, hidden by vines, was a narrow path leading into the trees. “The arrows point here!” <name> said excitedly.

They climbed carefully, and suddenly — splash! — a misty waterfall appeared, glimmering in the sunlight. The water sparkled like silver ribbons, and small colorful birds fluttered above it. “It’s beautiful!” gasped <name>.
At the base of the waterfall, they found smooth pebbles arranged in a spiral, just like the carvings on the rock. “Maybe someone made this to guide us here,” <name> whispered.

Miss Renu nodded. “And maybe they wanted others to discover how nature hides her secrets.” The class clapped and dipped their hands into the cool water. <name> smiled proudly. “We solved the mountain mystery — together!”
As they walked back, the sound of the waterfall followed them — whoosh-shhh — like a song of friendship and courage carried by the wind.
